response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior software developer with broad full-stack and some systems-level background, but he does not meet several hard requirements for this role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (languages listed are English, Romanian, German), his C/UNIX experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments, and the resume shows no Linux kernel modification work, no L2/L3 networking or switch experience, and no security-technology experience such as MACsec or 802.1X. His strengths lie in web, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang application development, which are largely orthogonal to embedded LAN-switch firmware security development.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development experience across diverse domains
Some low-level/systems exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD) and open-source contributions
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability — a hard requirement (native-level)
No embedded networking, L2/L3 switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
Minimal recent C/UNIX and no Linux kernel source-modification experience
